strengths: Well-conceived Damodaran methodology with explicit story-to-numbers-to-value chain
Detailed adversarial review design with clear verdict taxonomy: PASS/PASS-WITH-NOTES/REVISE/BLOCK and max-2-rounds revision loop
Pipeline phases follow a sound logical sequence: archetype to industry+theme to company to assumptions to engine to durability+triangulate to charts to report to audit to PDF
report_lint.py is a fully implemented production-quality mechanical linter with FAIL/WARN severity and --strict mode
References directory contains 15 substantive methodology documents covering playbooks per archetype, lenses, critique rubric, voice standards, and audit gates
WAVE parallelism correctly identifies the two genuine parallelization opportunities (industry+theme at WAVE 1, durability+triangulate at WAVE 5)
Escalation rules are clear: max 2 review rounds, max 3 pipeline iterations, never silently pass, publish with risk annotation

weaknesses.location: SKILL.md lines 39-57 Architecture tree and all pipeline phases
weaknesses.description: All 16 sub-skills in the architecture tree (classify-archetype through refresh-valuation) have NO corresponding SKILL.md files. Every directory is missing. The orchestrator cannot route to any sub-skill agent. The pipeline is a specification without implementation.
weaknesses.suggestedFix: Create each sub-skill directory with a SKILL.md defining inputs/outputs, self-check checklist, and review criteria. Alternatively restructure to use shared Python scripts directly and remove the agent-routing abstraction until it is real.

weaknesses.location: SKILL.md lines 27-31 and 233-239; loop-protocol.md Section 6 lines 259-276
weaknesses.description: SKILL.md claims Type-B gates route to cross-model (Codex xhigh) for verdict. But loop-protocol.md Section 6 states 3 of 5 same-family-safe conditions are unmet: Type-B gates are self-judged, verdicts not saved as artifacts, reviewer is same model family. Cross-model mechanism doesnt exist.
weaknesses.suggestedFix: Either build Codex API integration with verdict artifact round-trip or downgrade SKILL.md to state cross-model is aspirational and describe the self-review fallback honestly.

weaknesses.location: SKILL.md line 48 architecture tree and line 341 Phase 6 triangulate review
weaknesses.description: SKILL.md says /triangulate cross-checks 7 lenses and review asks 'all 7 lenses present?'. references/valuation-lenses.md documents only 4 lenses: Intrinsic DCF, Reverse DCF, Relative comps, Scenario thematic.
weaknesses.suggestedFix: Correct both mentions from 7 to 4 lenses or add 3 more lenses to valuation-lenses.md (break-even, sum-of-parts, liquidation value) to reach 7.

weaknesses.location: SKILL.md lines 215-232 Type-B gate table; loop-protocol.md lines 43-56 and 282-298
weaknesses.description: SKILL.md groups Type-B gates at high level (adversarial review catch-all) plus 3 specific ones (self-audit, write-report voice, charts visual). Loop-protocol.md breaks 7-dim self-audit into 4 individual Type-B gates and adds Currency sweep BREAKS verdict absent from SKILL.md. Also generate-charts visual quality appears in SKILL.md Type-B table but not in loop-protocol.md Type-B table. Both documents have different gate maps.
weaknesses.suggestedFix: Reconcile the two gate taxonomies. Either loop-protocol.md should be the single source of truth referenced by SKILL.md, or SKILL.md must mirror the full detail from loop-protocol.md.

weaknesses.location: SKILL.md lines 226-231 Type-B gate table and lines 399-404 Phase 9 PDF wave
weaknesses.description: Phase 9 (WAVE 8) describes adversarial review for /generate-pdf with specific criteria (charts embedded, CJK fonts, rating box, page numbers, disclaimer, B/W printable). But generate-pdf adversarial review does not appear in the SKILL.md Type-B gate table. It is listed in loop-protocol.md gate map at line 298.
weaknesses.suggestedFix: Add a generate-pdf Type-B entry to the SKILL.md gate table or expand the adversarial review catch-all to make coverage explicit.

weaknesses.description: Adversarial review (every sub-skill) is listed as a catch-all Type-B gate, but write-report voice/depth and generate-charts visual quality are redundantly listed as separate Type-B entries when they are already covered by the catch-all item.
weaknesses.suggestedFix: Either remove the redundant specific entries or replace the catch-all with explicit per-sub-skill entries for clarity.

weaknesses.description: The loop protocol describes a LOOP-STATE.md baton file with turn transitions, gate history, revision tracking, and escalation records. There is no code to create, update, validate, or parse this file. It is a documentation concept without enforcement.
weaknesses.suggestedFix: Either implement a Python module to manage LOOP-STATE.md (init/tick/gate_record/escalate functions) or remove the claim that state persists and note it as manual protocol.

weaknesses.description: Pipeline uses WAVE numbering 1-8 but inserts WAVE 5.5 between 5 and 6 for chart generation. This suggests the charts step was added after initial design and the numbering wasnt re-indexed. The architecture tree does not reflect wave numbers at all (it is a flat list).
weaknesses.suggestedFix: Re-index waves 5.5 to 6, 6 to 7, 7 to 8, 8 to 9. Or explicitly justify the 5.5 label as intentional (charts are a parallelizable dependency of report writing).

weaknesses.location: SKILL.md lines 418-452 Mode B and Mode C sections
weaknesses.description: Mode B (Critique) and Mode C (Refresh) mention adversarial review but do not classify their gates as Type-A or Type-B, do not specify cross-model routing, and do not define convergence/iteration limits. Only Mode A gets full protocol treatment.
weaknesses.suggestedFix: Add Type-A/B classifications and convergence rules to Mode B and Mode C sections, or explicitly state they inherit the same protocol from Mode A.

weaknesses.description: SKILL.md claims scripts/ contains Python valuation engine (10 programs). The scripts directory actually contains 21 Python files including chart builders, CSS generators, CJK font detection, and report_lint which are not strictly valuation engine programs. The count claim is stale.
weaknesses.suggestedFix: Update to reflect actual count or be more precise: e.g., 9 valuation engine scripts plus 8 chart builders plus linting.

weaknesses.description: SKILL.md says references/ contains 14 docs. There are actually 15 reference markdown files.
